navigation.groupScopeContent: Applies to {{scopeRoot}} only. Useful for group-specific timeouts and integrations.
navigation.globalScopeContent: Applies to your whole CCCC instance (daemon + Web). These settings affect all groups unless overridden.
navigation.globalLockedContent: Global settings are admin-only. Open Settings from a group to manage group-scoped settings, or sign in with an admin Access Token.

actorProfiles.name: Profile Name
actorProfiles.runnerModeHint: Use PTY for terminal interaction. Use Headless for structured event flows.
actorProfiles.runnerModeHeadlessNote: Only codex and claude support Headless mode in standard Web. Other runtimes stay on PTY.
actorProfiles.commandOverrideRequired: Command override is enabled. Please enter a command.
actorProfiles.customRuntimeCommandRequired: Custom runtime with PTY requires a command.
actorProfiles.capabilityDefaultsHint: Set startup autoload capabilities for actors using this profile.
actorProfiles.envHint: Stored as profile secrets. Values are never shown in plain text.
actorProfiles.duplicateSecretsHint: Duplicating from "{{source}}": existing variable keys are shown here and will be copied on save.
actorProfiles.secretsHint: Write-only and stored locally under CCCC_HOME/state/secrets. Hover configured keys to preview masked values.
actorProfiles.deleteInUseConfirm: This profile is still linked by {{count}} actor(s).
actorProfiles.deleteInUseForce: Delete anyway and convert linked actors to custom?
actorProfiles.myProfilesLoginRequired: Sign in to manage your personal profiles.
actorProfiles.scopeSecretsUnavailable: Secret env variables are not wired for this scope yet. Clear secret edits before saving.
actorProfiles.saveSecretsFailed: Profile saved, but failed to update secrets.

branding.title: Branding
branding.description: Customize the instance name and core logo surfaces without turning Web into a full white-label theme system.
branding.previewFaviconCustom: Browser tab icon is using a dedicated favicon.
branding.previewFaviconFollow: Browser tab icon follows the current logo icon unless you override it.
branding.productNameHint: Used for the sidebar wordmark, sign-in screen title, and browser tab title.
branding.logoHint: Shown in the sidebar header and sign-in screen. SVG or PNG is recommended.
branding.faviconHint: Used for the browser tab. If unset, it follows the logo icon automatically.
branding.faviconReset: Favicon now follows the logo icon again.

capabilities.title: Capability Governance
capabilities.subtitle: Global rules, sources, and registry for built-in and external capabilities.
capabilities.pageGuide: Global governance only. Configure external policy, manage sources, and inspect registry state. The self-evolving skill card below manages generated candidates without asking users to run tests.
capabilities.selfProposedHint: Agent-generated procedural skills appear here after import. Use this view to edit them, remove them, or choose which actors use them.
capabilities.selfProposedNoCandidates: No self-proposed skill candidates have been imported yet.
capabilities.manageSubtitle: Edit the generated skill text and choose which actors use it automatically.
capabilities.manageRequiresGroup: Open this panel from a group before saving or enabling a self-proposed skill.
capabilities.manageMissingCandidate: The selected self-proposed skill is no longer available.
capabilities.manageInvalidNamespace: Self-proposed skill ids must start with skill:agent_self_proposed:.
capabilities.manageActorLoadFailed: Failed to load actors for this group.
capabilities.manageBlockedBanner: This skill is blocked. Agents cannot use it until it is unblocked, but the record stays editable.
capabilities.manageRemoveConfirm: Remove this self-generated skill from the local library? Agents will no longer see or use it, and runtime/autoload references will be cleaned.
capabilities.manageCurrentUseHint: Direct actor assignments plus any temporary, group-wide, or profile-inherited use.
capabilities.manageNoCurrentUse: Not used automatically in this group.
capabilities.manageUsageGroup: All actors in this group · {{count}} actors
capabilities.manageUsageBlocked: Blocked skills cannot be used by agents until unblocked.
capabilities.manageActorAssignmentsHint: Check the actors that should use this skill automatically. Saving replaces group-wide use with this list and removes temporary runtime use from unchecked actors.
capabilities.manageAutoloadHint: Use the checkboxes below to choose which actors will automatically use this skill. Profile-inherited use is shown for awareness and managed in Actor Profiles.
capabilities.manageProvenanceHint: Where this generated skill came from, when it was last imported, and whether agents may use it.
capabilities.manageOtherActionsHint: Clear actor assignments for normal non-use. Block only when the skill is wrong, stale, risky, or should not be used by agents.
capabilities.manageDuplicateHint: Another self-proposed skill has the same name or slug. Prefer updating the existing record instead of creating near-duplicates.
capabilities.manageNoGroupHint: Viewing is available here, but save/enable/autoload actions require a concrete group.
capabilities.sourcesHint: Manage external capability feeds and inspect their sync health.
capabilities.libraryHint: Browse known capabilities, inspect readiness, and manage global block rules.
capabilities.requireGroup: Open this tab from a group to update block/unblock.
capabilities.blockedListHint: Capabilities blocked at the global level.
capabilities.failedSafetyMode: Failed to update external capability safety mode.
capabilities.safetyModeHint: Controls the default actionability of external capabilities. Built-in packs are unaffected.
capabilities.safetyModeCurrentRule: External sources currently follow the {{mode}} posture.

normal.label: Standard
normal.hint: External sources are usable by default, subject to per-source and per-capability overrides.

conservative.label: Conservative
conservative.hint: External sources default to a stricter indexed posture until explicitly relaxed.

readiness.blockedBySafetyMode: Blocked by conservative external import safety mode.

next.switch_external_capability_safety_mode_o: Relax safety mode or adjust allowlist

groupSpace.openFromGroup: Open this tab from a group.
groupSpace.title: Notebook Binding
groupSpace.description: Connect Google and choose the notebooks used for Work and Memory. Agents handle the rest.
groupSpace.loadFailed: Failed to load notebook binding state.
groupSpace.accountHint: Connect your Google account before selecting notebooks.
groupSpace.connectStartFailed: Failed to start Google connect flow.
groupSpace.switchStartFailed: Failed to start Google account switch flow.
groupSpace.connectCancelFailed: Failed to cancel Google connect flow.
groupSpace.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect the current Google account? This clears the local saved sign-in state but does not unbind Work or Memory notebooks.
groupSpace.disconnectFailed: Failed to disconnect the Google account.
groupSpace.disconnectBehaviorHint: Disconnect only clears the local Google sign-in state on this machine. Work and Memory bindings stay as they are.
groupSpace.workNotebookHint: Used for shared project knowledge and working materials.
groupSpace.noNotebookOptionsHint: No notebooks were found yet. You can create and bind a new one.
groupSpace.connectGoogleFirst: Connect Google first to manage notebook bindings.
groupSpace.adapterNotReady: NotebookLM is connected but not ready on this machine yet.
groupSpace.savedTargetHint: This lane still has a saved notebook target. Reconnect Google to use it again, or unbind it here if you want to clear it.
groupSpace.bindSelectedHint: Use the currently selected notebook for this lane.
groupSpace.bindSelectedHintWithTarget: Use the currently selected notebook for this lane: {{target}}
groupSpace.createAndBindHint: Create a new notebook and bind it to this lane right away.
groupSpace.unbindHint: Stop using the current notebook in this lane.
groupSpace.workUnbindConfirm: Unbind the current Work Notebook? Agents will stop using it until another notebook is bound.
groupSpace.memoryUnbindConfirm: Unbind the current Memory Notebook? Agents will stop using it for memory recall until another notebook is bound.
groupSpace.notebookSectionLockedDisconnected: Sign in with Google to view, bind, and manage Work and Memory notebooks.
groupSpace.notebookSectionLockedConnecting: Finish the current Google sign-in flow and the Work and Memory notebook controls will appear here.

automation.title: Automation
automation.description: Configure built-in automation and scheduled rules. Settings are stored in
automation.openFromGroup: Open this tab from a group.
automation.rulesDescription: Define scheduled reminders, operational actions, and reusable message snippets.
automation.versionConflict: Automation rules were updated elsewhere. Latest version loaded; please reapply your edits.
automation.versionConflictShort: Automation rules were updated elsewhere. Latest version loaded.
automation.noCompletedToClear: No completed one-time reminders to clear.
automation.resetConfirm: Reset automation rules and message snippets to defaults? This replaces your current setup.
automation.builtInSnippetReserved: "{{id}}" is a built-in snippet id. Edit or reset it instead of creating a custom snippet with the same name.
automation.snippetInvalid: Snippet name is invalid. Use letters/numbers/_/- (max 64 chars).
automation.editHint: Edit opens full rule settings in a modal. Snippets are managed here for reuse across rules.
automation.draftHint: Changes apply to draft immediately. Click Save in Rules & Snippets to persist.
automation.resetTitle: Reset rules and snippets to defaults
automation.operationalActionsHint: Set Group Status and Control Actor Runtimes are available only for One-Time schedule.
automation.createSnippetFirst: Create at least one snippet before selecting Snippet.
automation.operationalActionsOnly: Operational actions are available only with One-Time schedule.
automation.noSnippetsYet: No snippets yet. Create one in Snippets first.
automation.messagePlaceholder: Write the message sent when this rule runs.
automation.groupStateActiveHint: Start runners if needed, then resume active automation.
automation.groupStateIdleHint: Keep sessions running but disable proactive automation.
automation.groupStateStoppedHint: Stop all actor runtimes for this group.
automation.varHelpIntervalMinutes: Minutes for interval schedules (0 when not interval based).
automation.validationIntervalMin: Rule "{{id}}": repeat interval must be at least 1 second.
automation.validationOneTimeRequired: Rule "{{id}}": one-time send time is required.
automation.validationScopeInvalid: Rule "{{id}}": scope must be group or personal.
automation.validationOwnerRequired: Rule "{{id}}": personal rules require an owner.
automation.validationRecipientRequired: Rule "{{id}}": please select at least one recipient.
automation.validationSnippetMissing: Rule "{{id}}": message snippet "{{snippet}}" does not exist.
automation.validationMessageRequired: Rule "{{id}}": choose a message snippet or enter message text.
automation.validationGroupStateOneTimeOnly: Rule "{{id}}": Set Group Status only supports One-Time schedule.
automation.validationGroupStateTargetRequired: Rule "{{id}}": group state action requires active/idle/paused/stopped.
automation.validationActorControlOneTimeOnly: Rule "{{id}}": Control Actor Runtimes only supports One-Time schedule.
automation.validationActorControlTargetRequired: Rule "{{id}}": actor control requires at least one target.
automation.resetGuidanceConfirm: Reset {{kind}}? This will delete {{filename}} override under CCCC_HOME. This cannot be undone.
automation.removeRegistryConfirm: Remove {{count}} missing group entries from registry?

policies.title: Built-in Automation
policies.description: System-managed follow-ups and collaboration health loops. Adjust values, then save.
policies.unreadFollowupHelp: Remind a member when unread messages sit too long.
policies.digestMinGapHelp: Minimum gap between digests for the same member.
policies.maxRepeatsHelp: Maximum follow-ups for one pending item.
policies.escalateAfterHelp: Escalate when repeat count reaches this value.
policies.keepaliveDelayHelp: Wait time after an actor says 'Next:'.
policies.actorIdleAlertHelp: Alert foreman if actor is inactive for this long.
policies.groupSilenceCheckHelp: Two-step heuristic. The first timeout prompts the foreman to review; the second consecutive timeout sets the group idle. Set 0 to disable.

ruleEditor.oneTimeOnly: This action supports one-time scheduling only.
ruleEditor.recurringHint: Recurring: run daily, weekly, or monthly.
ruleEditor.oneTimeHint: One-Time: run once by countdown or exact time.
ruleEditor.personalRule: Personal rule (owner: {{owner}}). Scope is controlled by permissions.

ruleList.noRules: No rules yet. Create one or reset to defaults.

snippetModal.title: Snippets
snippetModal.description: Reusable notification messages for automation rules. Built-in snippets can be edited and reset, but not deleted.

delivery.title: Delivery
delivery.description: Control what happens after a PTY delivery succeeds.
delivery.autoMarkReadHelp: When enabled, successful PTY delivery advances the read cursor automatically. When disabled, messages remain unread until they are marked read explicitly.

messaging.title: Messaging
messaging.description: Configure how messages are routed when no explicit recipient is selected.
messaging.defaultRecipientDescription: Applies when <0>to</0> is empty (Web send with no recipients, IM <0>/send</0> with no <0>@targets</0>, MCP/CLI/SDK without <0>to</0>).
messaging.tip: Tip: Use explicit recipients for unambiguous routing.

imBridge.title: IM Bridge
imBridge.description: Connect this group to Telegram, Slack, Discord, Feishu/Lark, DingTalk, or WeCom.
imBridge.botTokenHintSlack: Paste xoxb-… token or an env var name; required for outbound messages.
imBridge.botTokenHint: Paste the bot token or an env var name; required for bot authentication.
imBridge.appTokenHint: Optional; needed for inbound messages (Socket Mode).
imBridge.feishuRegionHint: Feishu and Lark share the same APIs but use different domains. Pick the one where your app was created.
imBridge.feishuPackageHint: Inbound streaming requires the Python package <0>lark-oapi</0> on the host running CCCC.
imBridge.appIdHint: App ID value or an env var name.
imBridge.appSecretHint: App Secret value or an env var name.
imBridge.appKeyHint: App Key value or an env var name.
imBridge.robotCodeHint: Optional; used when the session webhook is unavailable or expired.
imBridge.weixinAccountIdPlaceholder: Default to the first logged-in Weixin account
imBridge.weixinAccountIdHint: Optional; kept mainly for legacy compatibility. The current implementation uses per-group cached `wechatbot-sdk` credentials.
imBridge.weixinPackageHint: The host needs the Python package `wechatbot-sdk`. After you start QR login, the page should switch to logged-in automatically once the scan succeeds.
imBridge.weixinStatusLoadFailed: Failed to load Weixin login status.
imBridge.weixinStartFailed: Failed to start Weixin QR login.
imBridge.weixinLogoutFailed: Failed to log out from Weixin.
imBridge.weixinHintIdle: Click Start QR Login to generate a scannable QR code here.
imBridge.weixinHintWaitingScan: Use WeChat's built-in scanner. If nothing happens for a while, regenerate the QR code.
imBridge.weixinHintLoggedIn: This account is connected. Regenerate the QR code or log out if you need to switch accounts.
imBridge.weixinHintError: The login flow failed. Regenerate the QR code and try again.
imBridge.weixinScanHint: After you click Start QR Login, a scannable QR code will appear here. Use WeChat's built-in scanner; if nothing happens for a while, regenerate the QR code.
imBridge.weixinSubscribeNeedsConfigBody: Save this IM Bridge config and start the bridge first. Then send <0>/subscribe</0> in the target Weixin bot chat.
imBridge.weixinSubscribeNeedsRunningBody: Start the bridge first. Then send <0>/subscribe</0> in the target Weixin bot chat; the request will appear in Pending Requests below.
imBridge.weixinSubscribeNextBody: Send <0>/subscribe</0> in the target Weixin bot chat. The request will appear in Pending Requests below; approve it before that chat can receive CCCC messages.
imBridge.weixinSubscribeBoundBody: Bound Weixin chats: {{count}}. To add another chat, send <0>/subscribe</0> in the new Weixin bot chat.
imBridge.dingtalkPackageHint: Inbound streaming requires the Python package <0>dingtalk-stream</0> on the host running CCCC.
imBridge.requestHint: Step 1: In your IM chat, send /subscribe to request a temporary key. Step 2: the request will appear below in Pending Requests; click Approve (or paste the key in Bind). If foreman is online, you can forward the key and ask foreman to bind it for you.
imBridge.requestCopied: Copied /subscribe. Send it in your IM chat to request a key, then approve it from Pending Requests (or bind by key).
imBridge.bindError: Key does not exist or has expired.
imBridge.verboseOnHint: Receiving all messages. Click to receive user-only messages.
imBridge.verboseOffHint: Receiving user-only messages. Click to receive all messages.
imBridge.setupStep1: Create a bot on your IM platform
imBridge.setupStep2: Set the token(s) as environment variable(s)
imBridge.setupStep3: Save the config and start the bridge
imBridge.setupStep4: In your IM chat, send /subscribe to receive messages

transcript.title: Terminal transcript
transcript.description: Readable tail for troubleshooting. User can always view; agent access is controlled by the policy below.
transcript.visibilityTip: Tip: This only affects agents. The Web UI user can always view transcripts.
transcript.tailViewerHint: Best-effort transcript tail from the actor PTY ring buffer.

guidance.openFromGroup: Open this tab from a group.
guidance.preambleHint: Injected automatically before the first delivery after start/restart.
guidance.resetHint: Delete override file and fall back to built-in
guidance.helpEditorHint: Structured mode edits common, role, and actor notes; raw mode keeps full-file control.
guidance.commonNotesHint: Untagged help content shared by all actors.
guidance.commonNotesPlaceholder: Keep shared guidance, workflow details, and appendices here…
guidance.foremanNotesHint: Only foreman actors receive this scoped block.
guidance.foremanNotesPlaceholder: Own outcome quality, review peer outputs, and keep shared direction coherent…
guidance.peerNotesHint: Only peer actors receive this scoped block.
guidance.peerNotesPlaceholder: Report risks early, deliver verifiable outputs, and say when the direction is wrong…
guidance.actorNotesHint: Local notes for specific actors. This is the same source edited from the actor modal shortcut.
guidance.actorNotePlaceholder: Describe only this actor's local responsibilities, boundaries, and preferred behavior…
guidance.noActorsForStructuredHelp: No actors available in this group yet.
guidance.orphanActorNotePlaceholder: Keep or clean this leftover note for an actor that no longer exists…
guidance.legacyRoleNotesHint: Legacy role notes were detected and mapped into the structured fields. Saving here will normalize them into scoped help blocks.
guidance.noScopeWarning: No scope attached to this group yet. Guidance overrides still work because they are stored under CCCC_HOME, but repo-linked features may stay unavailable until a scope is attached.

blueprint.openFromGroup: Open this tab from a group.
blueprint.exportDescription: Save this group's actors, settings, automation rules, and guidance as a single portable file.
blueprint.importDescription: Applies a blueprint by replacing actors, settings, automation rules/snippets, and group guidance overrides (CCCC_HOME). Ledger history is never modified.
blueprint.pickFileFirst: Pick a file to preview first
blueprint.replaceConfirm:
Replace this group's actors, settings, automation rules, and guidance using "{{filename}}"?

This will stop running agents and then apply group guidance overrides under CCCC_HOME:
- CCCC_PREAMBLE.md
- CCCC_HELP.md

developer.title: Developer Mode (Global)
developer.description: These settings apply to the whole CCCC instance (daemon + Web). Use this only when debugging.
developer.runtimeInfoHint: Quick version check for the current Web package and the connected daemon.
developer.versionMismatchHint: The Web package and daemon are on different versions. Restart CCCC to realign them.
developer.warningText: Developer mode enables verbose logs and extra diagnostics. Only enable it when you need it.
developer.enableHint: Enables debug snapshot and log tail tools.
developer.runtimeVisibilityHint: Controls which runtime panels appear in the standard Web UI.
developer.peerRuntimeHint: Shows or hides standard actor runtime tabs and terminal panels.
developer.petRuntimeHint: Shows or hides the internal pet runtime surface.
developer.terminalBuffersHint: Controls how much terminal history is kept. Larger buffers use more memory (scales with running actors and opened terminals).
developer.ptyBacklogHint: Affects initial history and transcript tail. Takes full effect after restarting an actor.
developer.webScrollbackHint: Controls how far you can scroll up in the Web terminal.
developer.registryDescription: Scan missing/corrupt groups in registry. Cleanup only removes missing entries.
developer.debugSnapshotHint: Shows daemon/actors state + delivery throttle summary (developer mode only).
developer.openFromGroup: Open Settings from an active group to view group-scoped debug info.
developer.logTailHint: Tails local log files (developer mode only). Component "im" is group-scoped.
developer.imLogsRequireGroup: IM logs require a group_id; open Settings from a group.

webAccess.title: Web Access
webAccess.description: Manage Web reachability, login, and interaction mode in one place.
webAccess.loadFailed: Failed to load Web Access state.
webAccess.publicUrlRequired: Public URL is required when the access goal is Public URL / tunnel.
webAccess.offProviderConflict: Provider=Off only makes sense with local-only binding. Choose the Local only preset, or switch provider to Manual/Tailscale first.
webAccess.saveFailed: Failed to save Web Access settings.
webAccess.applyFailed: Failed to apply the saved Web binding.
webAccess.applyReady: Saved. The running Web service is still on the old binding. Click Apply now to switch it.
webAccess.applyNotNeeded: The running Web binding already matches the saved configuration.
webAccess.restartRequired: Saved. Restart the running CCCC Web service to apply the new binding.

summary.localOnlyHint: The Web console is only reachable from this machine.
summary.applyPendingHint: The saved configuration changed, but the running Web service is still on the old binding. Click Apply now to switch it.
summary.applyPendingHintManual: The saved configuration changed, but the running Web service is still on the old binding. This launch mode cannot self-apply from the page and still needs an external restart.
summary.missingAccessTokenHint: Remote Web is configured to require an Access Token. Create one in Access Tokens, or turn off the token requirement for a trusted LAN.
summary.bindingUnreachableHint: The current Web host/public URL still does not produce a usable remote entry. Switch to 0.0.0.0, a specific LAN IP, or a Public URL.
summary.providerAuthRequiredHint: The remote provider still needs sign-in or machine authorization before Web can be exposed through it.
summary.providerNotInstalledHint: The selected remote provider is not installed on this machine yet.
summary.remoteEnabledHint: Remote access is available and ready to use.
summary.remoteNeedsAttentionHint: Remote access is configured but still blocked by a prerequisite or binding issue.
summary.openHint: No Access Tokens exist yet. Any browser on this machine can use Web until you create one.
summary.protectedHint: {{count}} Access Token configured. Browsers without a token must sign in, even on localhost.
summary.exhibitHint: The Web UI is running in read-only exhibit mode.

webAccess.accessControlDescription: Create admin or scoped Access Tokens. Scoped tokens can use only their assigned groups. Once at least one token exists, browsers without a token must sign in to use Web APIs, even on localhost.
webAccess.groupSelectionRequired: Select at least one allowed group for a scoped access token.
webAccess.firstTokenAdminHint: The first access token must be admin so you do not lock yourself out.
webAccess.adminTokenHint: Admin access tokens can manage global settings and other access tokens.
webAccess.scopedTokenGroupsHint: Scoped access tokens must be assigned to one or more groups. Global settings stay admin-only.
webAccess.customTokenPlaceholder: Leave empty to generate a secure random token
webAccess.newTokenHint: Copy and store this token now. The full value is not shown again in normal lists.
webAccess.noAccessTokens: No access tokens yet. The Web console is currently open by default on this machine.
webAccess.deleteConfirm: Delete this access token? This cannot be undone.
webAccess.reachabilityDescription: Reachability decides who should be able to reach Web. Access Tokens decide who can use it once they arrive.
webAccess.accessGoalDescription: Start with who should be able to reach Web. Binding and transport details stay under Advanced.

local.title: Local only
local.hint: Only this machine should open Web.
local.editorHint: Keep the simple local bind and just choose the port if you need a different one.

lan.title: Private network
lan.hint: Other devices on a private network should reach Web, either directly or through Tailscale.
lan.editorHint: Choose the private access method, the port, and whether this route should still require an Access Token.

public.title: Public URL / tunnel
public.hint: Web is reached through a reverse proxy, tunnel, or public URL.
public.editorHint: Set the externally visible URL here. Host/port stays available under Advanced.

webAccess.goalPortHintLocal: Use a different port only if another local service already occupies 8848.
webAccess.goalPortHintLan: Private-network peers will use this port once the running Web binding is applied.
webAccess.goalPortHintAdvanced: Only change the port here if your public proxy or tunnel needs Web to listen elsewhere.
webAccess.goalPublicUrlHint: Use the final browser URL that people should open, for example a reverse proxy or tunnel URL.
webAccess.publicTokenRequiredHint: As soon as a Public URL is set, CCCC forces Access Token protection for that route.
webAccess.primaryActionSaveHint: Save writes the new access goal and binding policy first. If the running Web socket still points at the old binding, you can apply or restart after saving.
webAccess.advancedDescription: Use this only when the default Local / Private / Public shapes are not enough, or when you need diagnostics.
webAccess.advancedDisclosureHint: Remote method, raw binding, diagnostics, and any Tailscale controls live here.
webAccess.wslHintBody: Inside WSL2, 0.0.0.0 usually exposes Web only inside WSL by default. For real LAN access, also enable mirrored networking or add a Windows portproxy/firewall rule.
webAccess.tailscaleDescription: Tailscale is a private-network method. It does not replace the saved Web binding by itself.
webAccess.presetsDescription: Pick a safe starting point, then fine-tune host and port if needed.
webAccess.presetLocalHint: Bind Web to 127.0.0.1 so only this machine can reach it.
webAccess.presetLanHint: Bind Web to 0.0.0.0 so localhost and other devices on your private network can reach it after restart.
webAccess.remoteMethodLanHint: Choose how this private-network route reaches Web: direct binding or Tailscale.
webAccess.privateMethodTailscaleHint: This private route will use your tailnet instead of plain LAN reachability. Advanced still contains the Tailscale start/stop controls and raw binding diagnostics.
webAccess.modeHintTailscale: The current Tailscale route is tailnet-only. There is no extra mode to configure here.
webAccess.webHostHint: 127.0.0.1 = this machine only. 0.0.0.0 = localhost plus your LAN IP. You can also enter one specific LAN IP.
webAccess.webPublicUrlHint: Optional. Use this when Web is reached through a reverse proxy or tunnel.
webAccess.requireAccessTokenHintLocal: Local-only binding does not need the remote exposure gate. Browsers still need to sign in whenever any Access Token exists.
webAccess.requireAccessTokenHintPrivateRequired: For LAN/private exposure, Access Tokens are required by default. Hosts can unlock insecure mode only via the advanced CCCC_REMOTE_ALLOW_INSECURE=1 override.
webAccess.requireAccessTokenHintPrivateOptional: For LAN/private exposure, keeping this on is strongly recommended. You can turn it off directly if this is only a trusted LAN.
webAccess.requireAccessTokenHintPublic: When a Public URL is set, Access Tokens are mandatory. Public or tunneled exposure should never run open.
webAccess.actionHintManual: Save only writes the Web binding policy. If switching local/LAN or changing host/port changes the bind address, restart the running CCCC Web service manually.
webAccess.actionHintMissingAccessToken: Remote Web currently requires an Access Token. Create one first, or uncheck the token requirement if this is only a trusted LAN.
webAccess.actionHintManualRestart: The running Web service is still on the old binding, but this launch mode cannot self-apply from the page. An external restart is still required.
webAccess.actionHintApplyReady: Save writes the target binding. Apply now performs a brief supervised Web restart and switches the running binding to the saved one.
webAccess.actionHintTailscale: Save only writes configuration. Start/Stop here control only the Tailscale remote side and do not rebind the currently running Web socket.
webAccess.noEndpoint: No remote endpoint is currently available.
webAccess.noNextSteps: No additional action is suggested right now.
webAccess.interactionModeDescription: Exhibit is a runtime launch mode, not another auth mechanism.
webAccess.modeGuideNormal: Normal mode is the standard interactive control plane.
webAccess.modeGuideExhibit: Exhibit makes the Web UI read-only for public or spectator viewing.
webAccess.modeGuideSwitch: To switch modes, restart Web with or without the exhibit flag.
webAccess.howItWorksActivateLogin: As soon as any Access Token exists, Web login becomes active for every browser session, including localhost.
webAccess.howItWorksAutoLogin: When you create the very first Access Token, this browser is signed in automatically with it.
webAccess.howItWorksRemotePolicy: Reachability and login are separate. Changing host/public URL affects network exposure; Access Tokens affect who can use Web once it is reachable.
webAccess.newTokenAutoLoginHint: This browser is already signed in with the new token.
webAccess.newTokenNoAutoLoginHint: Your current browser sign-in was kept unchanged. Use this token in another browser or sign in manually if needed.
webAccess.newTokenVerifyHint: Use an incognito window or another browser if you want to verify the login screen.
webAccess.currentBrowserOpenHint: No Access Tokens exist yet, so this browser does not need to sign in.
webAccess.currentBrowserSignedInHint: This browser is signed in as {{userId}} ({{role}}).
webAccess.currentBrowserNotSignedInHint: Login is active, but this browser is not signed in yet.
webAccess.currentBrowserCheckingHint: Login is active. This page is still refreshing the current browser session state.
webAccess.currentBrowserVerifyHint: Use an incognito window or another browser if you want to verify the login screen.
webAccess.managementLockedHint: This browser does not currently have admin access. Global settings, including Access Tokens, are locked until you sign in with an admin token.
webAccess.signOutHint: Sign out this browser and return to the Access Token login screen.
webAccess.restartDialogTitle: Restart Web to apply the new binding
webAccess.restartDialogBody: The new host and port were saved, but the running Web server is still listening on the old socket.
webAccess.restartDialogWhy: Changing host or port is a real socket rebind. Saving settings alone does not move the already running Web process.
webAccess.restartDialogLoginHint: If Access Token login is active, open the URL with ?token=<access-token> once or sign in in the browser.
webAccess.restartDialogCommandStandaloneLabel: If you normally run the Web server separately
webAccess.restartDialogSupervisorHint: If Web is managed by Docker, systemd, or another supervisor, restart that service instead of using the commands above.
